Hey, welcome back. It's time to get specific now with the strategy I've created to ensure that you build a massive stream of custom referrals, which means you'd be able to lower your lead cost dramatically. In other words, if you're getting a lot of referrals, not only do you cut down on the amount of leads you have to buy, but also your closing rate will go dramatically because let's just face it, referrals are a heck of a lot easier to close. And obviously that's because that second 10 has already been established and on some level the third 10 as well, and even the first 10 because when someone refers, so when someone else is saying, you know what, I've used that product, it's awesome. The sales person sells all that great and the company that stands behind it, well, you know, they're pretty good too. Remember that second 10 is like, wow, it's sky high because when someone refers you, they're essentially giving you that stamp of approval. Now, before we go any further here, let me just give you just one wacky fact here that I've actually researched on the internet, because I didn't seem to choose to actually dug into it. 27% of people out there who get sold things, might be give referrals. That's it. 27% give referrals. But when they were asked how many would give referrals, 90% of the people said they would. You get that? Less than a third are giving referrals, yet 90% said they would give referrals. Why is that? What's that gap about? You want to know what it is? People don't ask. The average sales person for whatever reason, whether they're too scared, they don't know how, they forget because it's not part of a script or a system, right? They never even ask. You know the old saying, you know, ask for and you shall receive all. Guess what? In this case, it is 1000% true. And what I've created here, I wouldn't really call it a system for referrals. It's really about a protocol. It's like a series of protocols of how you go about taking every single person you close and for that matter, even those that you don't close, right? And you ask them for referrals because what do you have to lose? Just understand this, that when you ask someone to refer you business, you do it in the right way. Let me get to teach you because there is obviously strategy for how I do it because I get more referrals than any 10 people I know, but there's a way that I do it. I ask in a certain way and also, and here's the big thing, by using the straight line system, when I close people, they're not just like kind of, they close because they had a low action threshold and I crossed over that action threshold. I might have a high one and I was able to loop and get past it. No, when I close, I've created airtight logical cases, airtight emotional cases. I approve myself to be such an expert at what I do that when it comes time to ask, I've earned the right. Now, I'm not saying that to say, oh, wow, Jordan, you're so good. No, I'm not trying to impress you about myself. I'm impressing upon you that everything that you've learned up to now, all of the straight line, it all feeds into this one possibility now, which is to take advantage of this simple yet wildly effective set of protocols I've created for turning all these new clients you've been able to close now using the straight line into a never-ending stream of referrals. You couldn't have done it if you closed with some usual half-baked system, you kind of either get lucky because they were born buyers or something like low action threshold people or they had some pain. I'm talking about you're going to be closed with them and they're done listening to you and they say, yeah, let's do it. They know exactly why. You've created an airtight logical case, airtight emotional case. You've earned the right to get referrals because they are comfortable. And all of this, this entire set of protocols, it starts with a first step, if you want to call that, and it might sound a bit shrike here, but it's actually the single most important thing you can do to ensure your long-term success in this area, pretty much with any other area as well, no matter what it is, and that is ready. You set a clear target for how many furrows you want to get each month and then you measure your results on a consistent basis. That's it. That's how it starts. You want to set a target. Don't leave it up to chance. You know, when I was out in the field, here's a perfect example. When I was out in the field doing mortgages and I built my business, A, by cold calling and B, massive referrals, right? I made it a point that said, okay, I know that there are certain types of people who are big referrals, accountants, lawyers, real estate brokers, right? I made it a point that as I was out there in the field cold calling, I said, I am going to hit a certain number of these people every single day. I'm going to make it a point that I am not stopping at how many sales I close. I'm going to hit a certain number of real estate agents every single day, a certain number of tax planners every single day, certain number of escrow attorneys every single day. Now, why would I do that? Simply put, because as you recall, sales is a numbers game. And if you remember from the lesson on prospecting with your sales funnel, what we do, we work backwards. I say to myself, well, how many furrows do I want to get each month? How many don't want to get? You know, I said a real, I said a target. What you don't set a target for, it's pretty hard to hit something. You don't know where it is even. You have to actually set a target. So that's my wide end. It's like I say, okay, here's my narrowing. I want to get extra furrows. What's my wide end? The people I speak to every single day. So I essentially reverse engineer my referral business and the same, we do anything else. And it starts at the end by saying how many referrals I want to get each month. And then essentially work way backwards to the front, which is how many people that the speak to every single day and ask for referrals. You get it. See, once you know all these ratios, okay, you backtrack from there, and it's actually pretty simple. It all takes care of itself because at the end of the day, sales is numbers game. And so is getting referrals. The more times you ask for referrals, the more referrals you're going to get. It's really simple stuff. Actually, this is not complicated. And with that, let's move on to our next protocol, which is number two. And that is when are you supposed to ask for a referral? Now, here's the deal. I'm going to give you one overarching concept here. Ready? As soon as possible, the biggest mistake that people make with this is they wait too long. They think that some arbitrary amount of time has to go by, like the customer's got to see all the benefits or make sure they made the greatest decision on earth. I can tell they're really ultimately happy. Like at that ultimate stage where like down the road, they've driven the car or use the product, they're feeling great and experiencing all the benefits, only then will they feel comfortable enough to give you a referral. Well, let me go back now to a story I've probably told twice already or three times in this training, because it's a great one. And that is about all the people whose homes I went to. And by the way, I always use the mortgage business, but it's also when I was selling meat and seafood and other products as well out in the field. But the typical scenario was I walked into someone's home and they're downright hostile to me. And then 15 minutes later, they're cooking me dinner, right? As I'm writing up the mortgage application or loading up their freezer with food, whatever it might be that I was selling, was selling them home, a law of service, whatever it might be, right? So all sorts of things. Now, as you can imagine, it didn't just stop with me getting dinner cooked for me. Every single time as I was writing up the application, I would ask for referrals and they would be on the phone nine times out of 10. That number 90 plus percent, it was true. Nine times out of 10, they'd be on the phone calling up their friends, their neighbors, their family, their coworkers, giving me as many referrals as they could. Why? Because I asked for them. Now, obviously, you're not going to get very far like that if you haven't been using the straight line in the way I've taught you, where you built massive rapport and you position yourself as that ultimate expert in your field. That is, because you position yourself a certain way, it all carries through. So when they buy from you, they bought as a result of that early positioning as an expert, which allowed you to then ask questions, identify their needs, right? Their pain points, and then resolve those pain points and the whole way through, they're like, wow, damn, this person's off. They really care about me. They feel my pain. They understand exactly what I need. They're experts in their field. Why would they not give a referral? See, understand the reverse psychology. Someone just buys something from you, right? Well, in the same way I said that when they upsell or cross sell, they're almost having consistency. They made the right decision. So they made another one to be consistent. Why wouldn't they refer a friend? They want to feel that they made the right decision. Far and few between of those, they will have to, have to see the whole thing for myself. I'm telling you, one out of 10, I don't feel comfortable. Let me first see for myself, 90% if you're a full right on the spot. Now, that being said, if you're in a B2B situation and it's, you know, a complex relationship, you might want to wait maybe a short time more, like after your first schmooze call or after your first upsell, but no longer than that. Listen, there is a time, you know, where you get into what's called either the love zone or the friend zone in life, right? With a woman or a man, right? You could also end up in the refer zone or the non-referrer zone. If you let it go too long and you don't ask referrals, you'll never get them. You're in the wrong zone. You want to be in the zone of the guy that gets the referrals. I'm just telling you, this is how life is. So you want to ask quickly for referrals, not slowly. So if you think that there is some arbitrary amount of time that has to go by, wipe that thought out of your head. It is untrue. Now, obviously there are certain key moments, like for instance, right after your close. Yeah, right after your close, I'm going to ask for referrals every single time. Okay? Right after I resolve a problem for someone, I'll ask for referrals. I'll take some that was bad, they have a problem and issue. They don't get on board correctly. And I solve that problem and say, oh, thank you so much, you're great. I'm going to ask them for a while. I'll tell you how I ask later on in a few minutes here, but I'm going to ask them right there. Okay, I'll ask them after I upsell them or cross sell them. I'll ask after they thank you for something. But there's these key times in the resource center. I'll give you a list of some of those key moments that are pretty obvious and then I have you list them out yourself based on your, I'll give you some prompts and have you list them out as well. Okay, but this is key here. Remember, you don't have to wait some arbitrary amount of time. People love to give referrals if they know you're the real deal. And I think, why would I do research? Because I'm saying, what is all this nonsense I'm reading about on the internet? Like you have to wait a certain amount of time. It's a big, it's just not true. Not for me, not for the people I train. They can get referrals very quickly because they've done the sale in a matter that is collaborative, that positions them as the absolute expert in the field and resolves people's pain. By doing that, you've earned the right to ask for referrals. And with that, let's stop here. We'll divide this referral lesson into two lessons. All right, so very quickly, take your quiz, go through each of the exercises because they're crucial here. And then when you come back, we'll top this off and finish up. I'll see you soon.
